title: Evasophy A to be 5 part evangelion analysis featuring philosophers such as Hegel Stirner Freud Zivek and more
display_title: Evasophy A to be 5 part evangelion analysis featuring philosophers such as Hegel Stirner Freud Zivek and more
wikitext:
[]: Evasophy - A to be 5 part evangelion analysis featuring philosophers such as Hegel, Stirner, Freud, Zivek and more             Quote Post by Xistence » Wed Sep 04, 2019 5:14 pm My friend is making a 5 part evangelion analysis series. He is big into post-modern philosophy and psycho analysis and whatnot, and we're 99% sure that Hegel (or his Japanese equivalent, forget his name) inspired eva. First part is about Hegel's world spirit vs Stirner's unique individuality. https://youtu.be/Dvk5BnMdXcc The second part is about Freud, psycho analysis and death drive. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5RxlCYeubBE

[]: Re: Evasophy - A to be 5 part evangelion analysis featuring philosophers such as Hegel, Stirner, Freud, Zivek and more             Quote Post by LPeyrani » Fri Sep 06, 2019 6:06 pm In my not-so-humble opinion, these videos are not interesting. The thinkers analized are well-known and their presentation is superficial, with the result of maximum boredom. It is apparent that there is no direct knowledge of the works of the authors but just of readings by others (see Zizek). Moreover, the connection to Eva is auxiliary and not structural. I won't go on, the intention was good and there's no reason to beat a dead horse. Tell your friend to study more (like... MUCH more), then start again and give us real "evasophy".

[]: Re: Evasophy - A to be 5 part evangelion analysis featuring philosophers such as Hegel, Stirner, Freud, Zivek and more             Quote Post by Gaijinnovideo » Sun Oct 27, 2019 8:25 am Thanks for sharing this, I’m really enjoying the Evasophy series. Its creator is succinct, dispassionate, and entertaining, all of which commentary on Eva could use more of. As someone who knows very little about philosophy, it’s been a helpful introduction to key thinkers and concepts. Plus there’s something oddly calming about hearing a cool, analytical German voice analyze Eva

[]: Re: Evasophy - A to be 5 part evangelion analysis featuring philosophers such as Hegel, Stirner, Freud, Zivek and more             Quote Post by Bryan » Thu Apr 02, 2020 1:45 am LPeyrani wrote: In my not-so-humble opinion, these videos are not interesting. It is apparent that there is no direct knowledge of the works of the authors but just of readings by others (see Zizek). Moreover, the connection to Eva is auxiliary and not structural. I won't go on, the intention was good and there's no reason to beat a dead horse. Tell your friend to study more (like... MUCH more), then start again and give us real "evasophy".
